title: Type and ownership visual grammar
summary: One balanced recursive grammar composes caret, optional, expected, array, string, and smart-pointer forms across every type position while expression operators keep their ordinary meaning.
details: Pointer declarator stars become carets only in type positions; multiplication and dereference stars remain stars.
The same segment grammar feeds declarations, flipped parameters, C-style and trailing returns, lambdas, raw Tree-sitter templates, and nested ordinary containers.
Exact bare or std::optional<T> becomes T?, exact expected<T, E> becomes T?E, and exact array<T, N> becomes [N]T; lookalike namespaces such as foo::optional remain verbatim.

Unique and shared pointers are presented as T^ with distinct ownership colors; weak pointers use T^? with a neutral caret and gold absence marker; a unique custom deleter is retained as a comma-delimited tilde arm.

Nested suffix and prefix expressions gain minimal structural parentheses whenever omission would collapse different source types to the same spelling: optional<unique_ptr<T>> is (T^)?, unique_ptr<array<T, N>> is ([N]T)^, and expected/optional compositions group their ambiguous arms.
Optional and expected question marks use the selected bold gold accent; std::nullopt retains its word spelling because the empty-set glyph rendered poorly in the owner's font.
const char* becomes CString and additional pointer levels remain carets; string, string_view, and configured zero-terminated string aliases share the string color.
Integer and floating C spellings normalize to the configured short aliases such as u32, i32, f32, and f64.
CUDA complex spellings follow DEC-005: cuFloatComplex and cuComplex become CUDA-colored cf32, and cuDoubleComplex becomes CUDA-colored cf64.
std::move and std::forward retain their operation names but are ownership-transfer colored; cpy marks an intentionally heavy by-value copy and copy joins the mutation/ownership color family.
Casts collapse to dollar aliases and pointer stars inside cast type arguments become carets.
Unsupported auto&& variable declarations stay raw, while forwarding references in parameters and range-for bindings have dedicated spellings.

provenance.note: Balanced recursive parsing produces spelling plus semantic roles, and every structured renderer consumes those chunks.
The outermost raw semantic template pass reuses the shared grammar, while aliases owns whole flipped-parameter ranges without overlapping smart-pointer shorthands.

evidence.reference: tests/frontend_spec.lua type, nested-template, pointer, cast, and ownership cases
evidence.claim: Nested and top-level type transformations are pinned.
Raw and overlay string/CString paths agree and avoid literal corruption.
A 5x5 wrapper-pair matrix, 125-row rendered corpus, reported constructor, color roles, exact namespaces, module toggles, and Visual lifecycle are pinned.

questions: Why does a pointer type use caret without changing multiplication?
How are optional, expected, arrays, and smart pointers rendered?

last_verified.method: Recursive grammar and every rendering consumer reviewed, then verified with focused interaction tests, a generated 125-row real-render corpus, style-lab RGB captures, and the full repository suite.
